ISA_200.title: Overall Objectives of the Independent Auditor
keyRequirements: Form audit opinion on financial statements
Conduct audit in accordance with ISAs

ISA_240.title: The Auditor's Responsibilities Relating to Fraud

ISA_315.title: Identifying and Assessing the Risks of Material Misstatement

ISA_320.title: Materiality in Planning and Performing an Audit

ISA_330.title: The Auditor's Responses to Assessed Risks

ISA_500.title: Audit Evidence
keyRequirements: Audit procedures (inspection, observation, inquiry, confirmation, recalculation, analytical)

ISA_560.title: Subsequent Events
keyRequirements: Events between year-end and audit report date (ISA 560)

ISA_700.title: Forming an Opinion and Reporting on Financial Statements
keyRequirements: True and fair view (Companies Act s393)

ISA_701.title: Communicating Key Audit Matters in the Independent Auditor's Report
keyRequirements: Identify KAMs (areas of higher assessed risk, significant management judgment)

2.title: Concepts and Pervasive Principles
2.disclosure: Qualitative characteristics (relevance, faithful representation, comparability, verifiability, timeliness, understandability)

3.title: Financial Statement Presentation
3.disclosure: Fair presentation, accounting policies, comparative information, going concern disclosures

8.title: Notes to the Financial Statements
8.disclosure: Accounting policy disclosure, critical judgments, key sources of estimation uncertainty

13.title: Inventories
13.disclosure: Valuation, cost methods (FIFO, weighted average), NRV testing, obsolescence

15.title: Revenue
15.disclosure: Recognition (performance obligations), timing of recognition, disclosures

16.title: Property, Plant & Equipment
16.disclosure: Initial cost, depreciation, useful lives, residual values, impairment

18.title: Intangible Assets & Goodwill
18.disclosure: Capitalization of development (6 criteria), amortization, impairment testing

20.title: Leases
20.disclosure: Finance vs operating (FRS 102 s20), ROU assets (IFRS 16), operating lease commitments

21.title: Provisions & Contingencies
21.disclosure: Provision criteria (obligation, probable, reliable), contingent liabilities, onerous contracts

23.title: Revenue (detailed)
23.disclosure: Stage of completion on long-term contracts, provision for losses

24.title: Government Grants
24.disclosure: Recognition timing, presentation (deferred income vs asset reduction), repayment risk

26.title: Share-Based Payments
26.disclosure: Fair value of share schemes, vesting conditions, expense recognition

28.title: Employee Benefits
28.disclosure: Pension schemes (defined benefit vs defined contribution), post-employment obligations

29.title: Income Tax
29.disclosure: Deferred tax (temporary differences, losses), current tax provision, disclosure

32.title: Financial Instruments
32.disclosure: Fair value, hedging, disclosure requirements, financial statement presentation

33.title: Related Party Disclosures
33.disclosure: Identification of related parties, transaction disclosure, key management compensation

s393.description: Financial statements must give true and fair view of assets, liabilities, financial position, and profit/loss
s393.auditImplication: Form opinion on true and fair view per ISA 700

s417.description: Directors must prepare strategic report (large companies) or directors' report (small/medium)
s417.auditImplication: Consider consistency with financial statements per ISA 720

s471-498.description: Format and content of financial statements (statement of financial position, P&L, cash flow, notes)
s471-498.auditImplication: Verify compliance with Companies Act s471-498 format requirements

s475.description: Requirement for auditor appointment, eligibility, removal, resignation
s475.auditImplication: Confirm auditor appointment per engagement letter

s485.description: Auditor's report on financial statements (similar to ISA 700)
s485.auditImplication: Audit report format per ISA 700 (UK) and Companies Act requirements

s488-489.description: Auditor must be independent; cannot provide certain non-audit services
s488-489.auditImplication: Confirm independence per FRC Ethical Standards ES1

s496.description: Audit working papers and file retained for 6 years minimum

ES1.title: Integrity, Objectivity and Independence
keyPoints: Threats to independence: self-interest, self-review, advocacy, familiarity, intimidation

ES3.title: Long Association with Audit Engagement
keyPoints: Key audit partner rotation (7 years max, then 2-year break)

ES4.title: Fees, Remuneration and Evaluation Policies
keyPoints: Audit fees not contingent on audit findings
Non-audit service fees limited to 70% of audit fee

ES5.title: Non-Audit Services Provided to Audited Entities
keyPoints: Prohibited services (tax/financial statement preparation, internal audit, management functions)
Restricted services (valuation, legal, recruitment, IT systems, bookkeeping)
Allowed services (compilation, compliance, general tax advice)

step1_EntityAndBusinessRisks.description: Identify risks that could impact the entity's ability to operate and achieve objectives
step1_EntityAndBusinessRisks.auditRelevance: Determine which business risks translate to risks of material misstatement

fraudTriangle.opportunity: Ability to commit and conceal fraud (weak controls, override)

commonFraudAreas: Expense cutoff and accruals (recording in wrong period)
Fixed asset disposals and impairment (avoiding losses)

step3_SignificantAccountingEstimates.description: Identify areas with significant management judgment (ISA 540)

step4_InherentRiskAssessment.description: Assess susceptibility of transaction or balance to material misstatement (before controls)

step5_ControlRiskAssessment.description: Evaluate design and operating effectiveness of controls (ISA 315/330)

testingApproach.designEffectiveness: Is control designed to prevent/detect misstatement?
testingApproach.operatingEffectiveness: Did control operate as designed throughout the period?

procedures: Control design and operating effectiveness testing

highRisk.auditApproach: Detailed substantive testing, possibly 100% testing or specialists

overallMateriality.definition: Threshold for planning, below which misstatements are not considered material
overallMateriality.selection: Use most relevant benchmark for entity's business model

performanceMateriality.definition: Threshold for designing detailed test procedures (typically 75% of OM)
performanceMateriality.purpose: Aggregate of undetected misstatements should not exceed PM

trivialThreshold.definition: Below which misstatements are not evaluated (typically 5% of OM)
